data.title: Advisor360 enhances Parrot AI for deeper client understanding in wealthtech, offering more capabilities to financial advisors.
data.description: In NEEDHAM, Massachusetts, an integrated wealthtech provider called Advisor360 has recently announced significant enhancements to its AI tool, Parrot AI, designed to assist financial advisors. The updates include additional features and increased flexibility for users.

data.title: Edible Garden raises $4.2M in warrant exercise deal, issues 4.04M new shares at $2.06/share. Expanding agriculture operations.
data.description: Today, Edible Garden AG Incorporated announced entering a warrant exercise agreement with an accredited investor. The agreement involves the exercise of 2,021,571 Existing Warrants to purchase common stock. In exchange for cash, the investor received 4,043,142 New Warrants. Additionally, the Company reduced the exercise price of the Existing Warrants to $2.06 per share, matching the recent closing price on the Nasdaq Capital Market. Edible Garden specializes in controlled environment agriculture, offering locally grown, organic, and sustainable produce and products.

data.title: Crimson Cup partners with couple to launch Horizons Coffee Bar in Bastrop, Texas, enhancing community vibes.
data.description: A pair has introduced a craft coffee hub with a focus on community in the rapidly expanding town of Bastrop, Texas. Horizons Coffee Bar, a mobile coffee shop located at 146 TX-21, commenced operations on August 1st. The establishment aims to bring people together through quality coffee and a communal atmosphere.

data.title: Terreno Realty buys property in South San Francisco for $5.6 million.
data.description: Terreno Realty Corporation has recently purchased a property located in South San Francisco, California for a total of $5.6 million. The acquisition was made in Bellevue, Washington.

data.title: US Orphan Drugs market expected to exceed US$190 billion by 2030 with over 850 clinical trials and 500 marketed drugs.
data.description: The US Orphan Designated Drugs market is expected to exceed US$190 billion by 2030. There are over 850 orphan drugs in clinical trials, with more than 500 already marketed. Over 400 marketed orphan drugs have data on sales, price, and dosage, with an extensive analysis available in more than 1000 charts and tables. The FDA's Orphan Drug Designation program plays a crucial role in developing therapies for rare diseases, presenting a lucrative opportunity in the orphan drug market. The full story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: HCW Biologics to present 3 posters on 3 lead drug candidates at 40th Annual Meeting of the Society for Immunotherapy of Cancer.
data.description: A presentation by the company will showcase research findings on three primary drug candidates developed using their innovative TRBC platform technology.

data.title: ATDev, based in Orange, receives $3 million Seed investment to expand assistive mobility offerings. #AT #AI #Funding
data.description: A company based in Orange, California named Assistive Technology Development, Inc. (ATDev) recently disclosed that they secured $3 million in Seed funding on October 16, 2025. ATDev specializes in AI-powered rehabilitation and personal mobility solutions. The funding round was headed by Dobrzelecki Legacy, highlighting investor interest in the company's innovative technologies and potential for growth.

data.title: Chartis Research names SAS a leader in 7 credit risk management categories, improving performance for financial services firms.
data.description: In the financial services sector, SAS has been recognized by Chartis Research as a Category Leader across seven sectors within the credit risk management market. This acknowledgment underscores SAS' commitment to providing a platform that is agile, efficient, and compliance-driven. The report was released on October 16, 2025, and highlights SAS' role in helping financial firms enhance their performance and decision-making processes. This recognition further solidifies SAS' position as a key player in the industry, demonstrating its strength in delivering innovative solutions.

data.title: Lightshift Energy and KeyBanc Capital Markets secure $75M facility for battery projects in US East Coast.
data.description: Lightshift Energy, a company specializing in battery energy storage projects in the U.S., and KeyBanc Capital Markets, the investment banking branch of KeyCorp, have finalized a $75 million credit facility. The funding will aid Lightshift in expanding their energy storage projects along the East Coast. This flexible facility comprises a term loan and construction-to-term loan. KeyCorp, listed on the NYSE as KEY, is based in Cleveland.
